title: Learn Effective Pit-Loss Priors From Realized Earlier Stops
summary: Official F1 strategy coverage emphasizes that even a few tenths of pit-loss can open the window earlier; the current solutions use coarse circuit priors but not a learned operational stop-cost proxy from prior realized stops.
rationale: Official F1 strategy coverage emphasizes that even a few tenths of pit-loss can open the window earlier; the current solutions use coarse circuit priors but not a learned operational stop-cost proxy from prior realized stops.
implementation_hint: Chronologically reconstruct earlier pit episodes and estimate realized stop-loss proxies from in-lap/out-lap and position change around previous `PitStop`s. Smooth these by `Driver`, `Race`, `Year`, and wet/dry regime, then add mean/variance/fast-stop indicators as features.
expected_effect: Should separate equally tyre-worn cars where one profile can afford the undercut and another cannot.
risk: Stop-loss measurement is noisy and can leak if any post-decision information is included incorrectly.
